predictions.text: China’s National People’s Congress (NPC) will set China’s 2026 official general public budget deficit target at or above 3.5% of GDP (as stated in the Government Work Report or Ministry of Finance budget report).
predictions.verification_criteria: Verification requires the official NPC-released Government Work Report (or official MoF budget report) explicitly stating a 2026 deficit-to-GDP target ≥ 3.5%.
evidence: China signals mixed momentum ahead of major policy meetings; February factory activity fell more than expected, complicating growth-support decisions. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/04/china-factory-activity-february-pmi.html
monitoring_signals: Draft/preview figures leaked ahead of NPC (deficit ratio, special bond quota, local government bond quota)
MOF press briefings and Xinhua summaries of the Government Work Report
Policy-bank or central-government announced stimulus packages tied to the Work Report
predictions.rationale: Weak factory momentum increases odds of a more expansionary fiscal stance; a ≥3.5% deficit target is a clear, observable threshold for meaningful incremental stimulus.

predictions.text: CISA, FBI, and NSA (or CISA + FBI) will publish a joint cyber advisory explicitly attributing malicious activity to Iran (e.g., IRGC-linked actors) targeting U.S. critical infrastructure (energy, water, transport, financial, or healthcare).
predictions.verification_criteria: A public advisory (Cybersecurity Advisory/CSA, Joint CSA, or equivalent) on cisa.gov/fbi.gov/nsa.gov that explicitly names Iran or an Iran-linked threat actor and explicitly states targeting of U.S. critical infrastructure sectors.
evidence: Cyber dimension intensifies: U.S. cyber agency stretched as Iran-linked threats rise. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/03/iran-cisa-cybersecurity-war-threat.html
monitoring_signals: CISA ‘Current Activity’ and ‘Cybersecurity Advisories’ pages for Iran-linked actor writeups
FBI FLASH/PSA items referencing Iran-linked actors
Significant reported intrusions affecting U.S. utilities or pipeline operators with Iran-linked indicators
predictions.rationale: Escalation periods often produce heightened Iran-linked cyber activity and corresponding U.S. government advisories; attribution/joint-publication is common when risk is broad.

predictions.text: At least one large U.S. private-credit interval fund sponsored by Blackstone, Apollo, Ares, or KKR will disclose that it fulfilled less than 90% of repurchase requests in a single repurchase period (i.e., pro-rated repurchases).
predictions.verification_criteria: A shareholder notice, prospectus supplement, Form N-PORT/N-CSR, website notice, or equivalent sponsor communication stating repurchase requests exceeded the offer amount and that investors received <90% of requested repurchase proceeds for that period.
evidence: Private credit strain: record redemptions at a major fund and concerns over risky lender loans spotlight liquidity mismatch risks. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/03/blackstone-private-credit-fund.html
monitoring_signals: Monthly/quarterly repurchase offer results posted by interval funds
Rising credit spreads and secondary private-credit NAV marks
Sponsor commentary about managing liquidity, scaling repurchase caps, or elevated repurchase demand
predictions.rationale: If redemption pressure persists, pro-ration events are a crisp, verifiable sign of liquidity strain in semi-liquid private-credit structures.

predictions.text: Apple will issue at least one iOS/iPadOS ‘Rapid Security Response’ or out-of-band security update where Apple’s security advisory states a vulnerability was ‘exploited in the wild’ and references ‘mercenary spyware’ (or equivalent language).
predictions.verification_criteria: An Apple Security Release note (support.apple.com) for iOS/iPadOS that (a) states ‘exploited in the wild’ (or ‘may have been exploited’) and (b) includes language explicitly referencing mercenary spyware (or closely synonymous phrasing).
evidence: Government-grade iPhone hacking tools reportedly leaked into criminal hands, raising systemic mobile security concerns. https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/03/a-suite-of-government-hacking-tools-targeting-iphones-is-now-being-used-by-cybercriminals/
monitoring_signals: Apple Security Releases page for RSR/out-of-band updates
Citizen Lab / Amnesty Security Lab reporting on iOS exploitation
Mandiant/CrowdStrike advisories linking iOS exploits to spyware ecosystems
predictions.rationale: Tool leakage increases the chance of real-world exploitation and emergency patching; Apple has precedent for explicitly calling out mercenary spyware in advisories.

predictions.text: Apple will report year-over-year Mac segment revenue decline in at least one quarterly earnings release filed with the SEC (10-Q/10-K) or in its earnings press release.
predictions.verification_criteria: Any Apple quarterly earnings release/10-Q/10-K before the deadline shows Mac net sales (or segment revenue) for the quarter lower than the same quarter one year prior (YoY negative).
evidence: Apple raises MacBook prices as M5 launch and RAM shortages signal an AI-first hardware cycle; higher device costs may pressure demand. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/03/apple-macbook-prices-m5-ai.html
monitoring_signals: Channel checks on MacBook sell-through and enterprise refresh cycles
Apple supplier commentary on RAM/component constraints and pricing
Consumer sentiment/retail electronics demand indicators
predictions.rationale: Higher prices and constrained components can suppress unit volumes; a YoY revenue decline is a clean, audited outcome measure.

predictions.text: U.S. core PCE inflation (price index excluding food and energy) will print at or above 3.0% year-over-year in at least one BEA Personal Income and Outlays release.
predictions.verification_criteria: A BEA release (tables for PCE price index) shows core PCE year-over-year change ≥ 3.0% for any month reported prior to the deadline (including revisions).
evidence: NY Fed’s Williams: tariffs are borne overwhelmingly by U.S. businesses and consumers, reinforcing inflation pass-through concerns. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/03/new-york-feds-williams-says-tariff-burden-falls-overwhelmingly-on-us-businesses-and-consumers.html
monitoring_signals: Monthly CPI/PPI core services and goods inflation prints
Updates on tariff implementation scope/timing and retailer pricing actions
Inflation expectations (UMich, NY Fed survey) and wage trackers
predictions.rationale: Tariff pass-through plus supply disruptions can lift core goods/services; 3.0% core PCE is a salient, high-signal threshold for policy debate.

predictions.text: The FOMC will cut the federal funds target range by at least 25 basis points (upper bound reduced by ≥0.25 percentage points) at least once.
predictions.verification_criteria: An FOMC policy statement shows the target range upper bound is at least 0.25 percentage points lower than it was on 2026-03-04.
evidence: Market stress rising as investors reprice war-risk, oil, and growth impacts. https://www.marketwatch.com/story/wall-streets-fear-gauge-is-rising-as-iran-conflict-escalates-heres-what-investors-should-watch-543b5d5c?mod=mw_rss_topstories

Tariff burden falls on U.S. businesses and consumers, complicating growth/inflation tradeoffs. https://www.cnbc.com/2026/03/03/new-york-feds-williams-says-tariff-burden-falls-overwhelmingly-on-us-businesses-and-consumers.html
monitoring_signals: Fed speakers shifting toward downside growth risks / financial conditions tightening
Unemployment claims trend and payroll surprises
SOFR futures implied path showing ≥1 cut priced with high probability
predictions.rationale: If growth slows under tariff and financial-conditions pressure, at least one cut by late Q3 becomes plausible even with elevated inflation uncertainty.

predictions.text: The U.S. Department of Energy will announce (press release or FOA) at least $200 million in new federal funding specifically for fusion energy R&D or fusion-enabling technologies (materials, plasma control, tritium handling, etc.).
predictions.verification_criteria: A DOE press release and/or FOA (energy.gov or grants.gov) explicitly labeled as fusion-focused that totals ≥$200,000,000 in available funding (single program or combined within the announcement).
evidence: Fusion commercialization may hinge on enabling technologies that improve plasma control and materials, potentially accelerating timelines. https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2026/03/260303050622.htm
monitoring_signals: DOE Office of Science and ARPA-E budget and new FOA postings
Congressional appropriations language referencing fusion lines
Major U.S. fusion demonstrations, pilot plant partnerships, or milestone achievements prompting funding expansions
predictions.rationale: Growing strategic interest and enabling-tech progress raise odds of a sizeable DOE funding tranche; $200M is a meaningful but attainable program scale.
